dtcs.description: SDM Internal Failure DTC Will be Set when An internal SDM fault is detected by SDM.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace SDM.

Repeat Air Bag Diagnostic System Check:Advanced Air Bag.  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Air Bag Diagnostic System Check Except Advanced Air Bag

dtcs.description: "AIR BAG" Warning Light Circuit Failure
"PASS AIR BAG OFF" Indicator Light Circuit Failure Step 1-3 Step 4-5
Power Source Voltage Too High Step 1-3
Power Source Voltage Low Step 1-3 Step 3 (Continued)-5
OCM-SDM Serial Communication Problem Step 1-4

Air Bag Module Exploded DTC Will be Set when The SDM detects a crash of sufficient force to warrant activation of the front (Driver, Passenger and both seat bolt pretensioner) air bag module. (SDM outputs a deployment command.) Flow Test Description STEP 2: Check that DTC B1021 has been set although front-air bag has not been deployed. STEP 3: Check that DTC has been set due to failure of SDM. Step 1-3

Driver / Passenger Side-Air Bag Module Exploded DTC Will be Set when The SDM detects a crash of sufficient force to warrant activation of the side-air bag module. (SDM outputs a deployment command.) Flow Test Description STEP 2: Check that DTC B1024 / B1025 has been set although side-air bag has not been deployed. STEP 3: Check that DTC has been set due to failure of SDM. Step 1-3

Pretensioner Module Exploded DTC Will be Set when The SDM detects a crash of sufficient force to warrant activation of the pretensioner module. (SDM outputs a activated command.) Flow Test Description STEP 2: Check that DTC B1026 has been set although pretensioner has not been activated. STEP 3: Check that DTC has been set due to failure of SDM. Step 1-3

Driver / Passenger Side Curtain Air Bag Module Exploded DTC Will be Set when The SDM detects a crash of sufficient force to warrant activation of the side curtain-air bag module. (SDM outputs a deployment command.) Flow Test Description STEP 2: Check that DTC B1028 / B1029 has been set although side curtain-air bag has not been deployed. STEP 3: Check that DTC has been set due to failure of SDM. Step 1-3

Driver / Passenger Side Forward Sensor Circuit Performance Problem DTC Will be Set when SDM receives internal fault signal or wrong ID (Part No.) signal from forward-sensor. Flow Test Description STEP 2: DTC check. Step 1-2
Driver / Passenger Side Forward Sensor Circuit Communication Error DTC Will be Set when Forward-sensor abnormal signal is detected by SDM. Flow Test Description STEP 2: DTC check. Step 1-2
Driver / Passenger Side Forward Sensor Circuit Short to Ground Step 1-3
Driver / Passenger Forward-Sensor Circuit Short to Power Circuit or Open Step 1-2 Step 2 (Continued)-3 Step 3 (Continued)-4

Driver / Passenger Side-Sensor Circuit Performance Problem DTC Will be Set when SDM receives internal fault signal or wrong ID (Part No.) signal from side-sensor. Flow Test Description STEP 2: DTC check. Step 1-2
Driver / Passenger Side-Sensor Circuit Communication Error DTC Will be Set when Side-sensor abnormal signal is detected by SDM. Flow Test Description Step 2: DTC check Step 1-2
Driver / Passenger Side-Sensor Circuit Short to Ground Step 1-3
Driver / Passenger Side-Sensor Circuit Short to Power Circuit or Open Step 1-2 Step 2 (Continued)-3
Wrong Side-Sensor ID DTC Will be Set when SDM receives wrong ID (Part No.) signal from side-sensor.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace driver or passenger side-sensor.

dtcs.description: Left / Right Side-Sensor Performance Problem DTC Will be Set when SDM receives internal fault signal from side-sensor.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace left or right side-sensor.

dtcs.description: Left / Right Side-Sensor Communication Error Step 1-2 Step 2 (Continued)-4
(No. 1133): Battery Voltage Too High Step 1
(No. 1141 / No. 1142): Outside Air Temperature (Ambient Temp.) Sensor Circuit Malfunction Step 1-2
(No. 1150): Air Bag Communication Circuit Malfunction Step 1-2
(No. 1157): Air Bag Deployment Signal Input Step 1-2
(No. 1170): EEPROM Access Error

steps.instruction: Ignition switch OFF.
Replace BCM.
Repeat BCM Check Flow Table.

dtcs.description: CAN Vehicle Speed Date Invalid DTC Will be Set when The SDM received invalid vehicle speed date signal from ECM for specified counts. Step 1-2
OCM ECU Fault DTC Will be Set When An internal OCM fault is detected by SDM.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM Sensor Mat Fault DTC Will be Set When SDM receives internal errors OCM electronics fault or/and sensor mat fault signal from OCM.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM Data Memory Damaged DTC Will be Set when FRAM memory in OCM can not be written or crash memory full.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM Battery Voltage Out of Range Step 1-4
OCM Temperature Out of Range DTC Will be Set When When OCM internal temperature reaches to out of the performance guarantee temperature (-20 °C - 65 °C (4 - 149 °F)).

steps.instruction: Leave the vehicle for about an hour in the outside temperature 10 - 30 °C (50 - 86 °F) with all windows opened.
Check that the surface temperature of the front passenger seat is between -20 °C (4 °F) and 60 °C (149 °F).
Turn off the ignition switch once. Then turn it on and check if a trouble code is detected. If it is detected, replace OCM.

dtcs.description: OCM Unexamined DTC Will be Set When OCM is not calibrated at product inspection at manufactory.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM Wrong ID DTC Will be Set When SDM receives wrong ID (Part No.) signal from OCM.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM Check Sum Error DTC Will be Set When SDM receives abnormality signal from OCM.

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace OCM with sensor mat.

dtcs.description: OCM External Faults Recorded DTC Will be Set When SDM has a history DTC for OCM (when DTC clearance has not been executed). Step 1-3

1388: Driver / Passenger Seat Belt Buckle Sensor Performance Problem

steps.instruction: Turn ignition switch OFF.
Replace defective seat belt buckle sensor.

dtcs.description: Low Tire Pressure (DTC B1411: Low Tire Pressure (Tire Pressure Sensor No.1) / ) (DTC B1412: Low Tire Pressure (Tire Pressure Sensor No.2) /) (DTC B1413: Low Tire Pressure (Tire Pressure Sensor No.3) /) (DTC B1414: Low Tire Pressure (Tire Pressure Sensor No.4)) Step 1-2

H/L: Inside Air Temperature Sensor and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC.

dtcs.description: H/L: Evaporator Temperature Sensor and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC.

dtcs.description: H/L: Sunload Sensor and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC. Step 1-4 Step 5-8

dtcs.description: H/L: Temperature Control Actuator (Position Sensor) and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC.

dtcs.description: H/L: Air Flow Control Actuator (Position Sensor) and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC.

dtcs.description: Temperature Control Actuator and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Start A/C system and select temperature selector at "MAX COOL" position or "MAX HOT" position.
Wait for 1 minute.
Check DTC. Step 1-5 Step 6-10 Step 11-12

dtcs.description: Air Flow Control Actuator and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Start A/C system and select mode selector at "DEF" position.
Select mode selector at "FOOT" position and wait for 1 minute.
Check DTC. Step 1-5 Step 6-10 Step 11-12

dtcs.description: H/L: Air Intake Control Actuator (Position Sensor) and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Check DTC. Step 1-4 Step 4 (Continued)-8 Step 9-13

dtcs.description: Air Intake Control Actuator and/or Its Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Start A/C system and select air intake selector at "FRE" position.
Select air intake selector at "REC" position and wait for 1 min. or more.
Check DTC. Step 1-5 Step 6-9 Step 10-11

dtcs.description: Back-Up Power Supply Circuit Malfunction Step 1-2
A/C Refrigerant Pressure Malfunction

steps.instruction: Connect scan tool to DLC with ignition switch turned OFF.
Turn ON ignition switch and clear DTC using scan tool.
Start engine and warm up to normal operating temperature.
Start A/C system for 1 min. or more.
Check DTC. Step 1-5
